# Implementation Plan: Mode-Aware QA and Developer Workflows

**Branch**: `002-mode-aware-workflows` | **Date**: 2026-05-11 | **Spec**:
[spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from
`/specs/002-mode-aware-workflows/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by `/speckit-plan`. It extends the existing
TypeScript/oCLIF Lark Bitable CLI without changing the read-only Lark access
model.

## Summary

Add an explicit workflow mode setting with two user-facing values, `QA` and
`Developer`. Developer mode preserves the existing bug list/search/filter/triage
and research workflow, while adding owner-aware discovery. QA mode adds
task-focused verification: it analyzes a selected Bitable task, inspects the
current workspace for safe check evidence, runs only evidence-backed safe checks
when possible, and writes a report that separates executed checks, skipped
checks, observed facts, assumptions, risks, and next actions.

The technical approach is to extend the existing local config model under
`~/.lark-bitable/config.json`, add mode-aware readiness/help/configure
behavior, centralize owner filtering in shared record discovery paths, and add a
new QA verification command that reuses the existing evidence/reporting
boundaries.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5.9.x on Node.js `>=22`, matching
`package.json`.
**Primary Dependencies**: Existing stack: `@oclif/core` and `oclif` for CLI
commands/help, `@inquirer/prompts` for guided setup and selection,
`@larksuiteoapi/node-sdk` for Lark OpenAPI reads, `conf` plus `zod` for local
configuration validation, `open` for SSO login, and `vitest` for tests.
**Storage**: Existing user-home store. Config and selected task state live under
`~/.lark-bitable/config.json`; Lark auth lives under
`~/.lark-bitable/auth.json`. This feature adds workflow mode, mode-specific
defaults, and owner settings to the config file. Secrets remain redacted and are
never written to reports.
**Testing**: Vitest unit and integration tests using fixtures and mocked Lark
responses. Live Lark verification remains a manual quickstart path because it
requires user credentials, app permissions, and Base access.
**Target Platform**: Developer workstations and AI-agent execution environments
that can run the Node CLI.
**Project Type**: Single TypeScript CLI package with AI-facing bootstrap skill
assets.
**Performance Goals**: Mode setup should add minimal guided prompts after
source/auth setup, and optional owner prompts may be left blank. Owner-filtered
discovery should process the same record page already fetched by
list/search/filter/triage without additional per-record network calls when an
owner field is configured. Query limit handling should cap returned records or
candidates after criteria and sorting are applied. QA verification should finish
workspace check discovery before running commands and should stream or
summarize command outcomes without hiding failures.
**Constraints**: Read-focused Lark behavior; no destructive Lark writes; no
source-code mutation from QA mode; no raw tokens in CLI output or reports; QA
checks run only when supported by workspace evidence and blocked when commands
look destructive, unrelated, or unsupported; all report conclusions must cite
evidence or be labeled as assumptions.
**Scale/Scope**: One CLI package. Update config schema/store/readiness/help,
configure prompts, shared record discovery, list/search/filter/triage/research
contracts, add QA verification/reporting modules, update bootstrap skill and
README guidance, and add tests for mode, optional owner filtering, owner-filter
not-applied output, query limits, QA validation, and evidence boundaries.

## Constitution Check

_GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design._

- **AI consumer contract**: PASS. The feature defines machine-readable mode,
  owner criteria, executed checks, skipped checks, evidence references, issues,
  and next safe commands. New command output must keep `status`, `issues`,
  `evidence`, and structured `data` sections aligned with existing
  `CommandOutput`.
- **Evidence sources**: PASS. Planning evidence comes from the user request,
  [spec.md](./spec.md), existing source files under `src/config`, `src/cli`,
  `src/triage`, and `src/reporting`, `package.json`, and previous planning
  artifacts under `specs/001-lark-bitable/`.
- **Unsupported-claim prevention**: PASS. The plan marks QA check selection as
  evidence-based and fail-closed. Reports must label unverified causes, skipped
  checks, and missing workspace evidence instead of converting them into facts.
- **Reproducible validation**: PASS. The design requires deterministic Vitest
  coverage for config migration, mode validation, owner matching, command
  output, and QA verification against fixtures. Live Lark behavior is documented
  separately as manual validation.
- **Report traceability**: PASS. QA and Developer reports must preserve observed
  facts, assumptions, analysis, executed command evidence, skipped checks, risks,
  and next actions as separate sections.

Post-design re-check: PASS. [research.md](./research.md),
[data-model.md](./data-model.md), [contracts/cli-contract.md](./contracts/cli-contract.md),
and [quickstart.md](./quickstart.md) preserve evidence boundaries, fail-closed
validation, token redaction, read-only Lark access, owner criteria traceability,
and reproducible test expectations.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/002-mode-aware-workflows/
├── plan.md
├── research.md
├── data-model.md
├── quickstart.md
├── contracts/
│   └── cli-contract.md
└── tasks.md
```

`tasks.md` is intentionally not created by `/speckit-plan`; it is the expected
output of `/speckit-tasks`.

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
package.json
README.md
src/
├── cli/
│   ├── commands/
│   │   ├── configure.ts
│   │   ├── valid.ts
│   │   ├── help.ts
│   │   ├── list.ts
│   │   ├── get.ts
│   │   ├── filter.ts
│   │   ├── search.ts
│   │   ├── triage.ts
│   │   ├── research.ts
│   │   └── verify.ts
│   ├── output.ts
│   └── shared-records.ts
├── config/
│   ├── auth-store.ts
│   ├── readiness.ts
│   ├── schema.ts
│   └── store.ts
├── lark/
│   ├── client.ts
│   ├── field-discovery.ts
│   ├── record-mapper.ts
│   └── url-parser.ts
├── mode/
│   ├── mode-config.ts
│   └── owner-filter.ts
├── qa/
│   ├── check-discovery.ts
│   ├── check-runner.ts
│   └── verification-report.ts
├── reporting/
│   ├── evidence.ts
│   └── research-report.ts
└── bootstrap/
    ├── installer.ts
    └── skill/
        └── SKILL.md

tests/
├── fixtures/
├── integration/
└── unit/
```

**Structure Decision**: Keep a single TypeScript CLI package. Add small
mode-specific modules only where they prevent command duplication:
`src/mode/owner-filter.ts` centralizes owner value extraction/matching, and
`src/qa/` contains QA verification discovery, execution, and report rendering.
Existing command adapters remain thin and continue to use shared config,
readiness, output, and record loading helpers.

## Complexity Tracking

No Constitution Check violations require justification. The design extends
existing CLI/config/reporting patterns and uses current dependencies instead of
adding a new framework.
